description: Fix skipPermissions state being reset on dev proxy hot reload. When the user clicks 'Restore approvals' in the side panel, the runtime skipPermissions=false value is lost when the dev proxy kills and forks a new worker — the new process re-reads the env var and reverts to skipPermissions=true. The dev proxy should track skipPermissions changes via IPC and pass the current value to the new worker.

userStories.title: Worker sends skipPermissions state changes to the dev proxy via IPC
userStories.description: When handleConfigSetSkipPermissions changes state.skipPermissions at runtime, the worker should notify the dev proxy via process.send so the proxy can track the current value and pass it to the next worker.
acceptanceCriteria: When state.skipPermissions changes (via config.setSkipPermissions handler), the worker sends an IPC message { type: 'skipPermissions', value: boolean } to the parent process
The IPC message is only sent when running behind the dev proxy (process.env.OPENTABS_PROXY === '1' && process.send)
The IPC message is fire-and-forget — errors sending are silently caught
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (send IPC after state change)
- platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts (reference for existing IPC pattern)

Context:
`handleConfigSetSkipPermissions` at extension-handlers.ts line 516 sets `state.skipPermissions = skipPermissions`. This is the only place where skipPermissions changes at runtime (triggered when the user clicks 'Restore approvals' in the side panel).

The worker already has an IPC channel to the proxy — index.ts lines 232-233:
```typescript
if (process.env.OPENTABS_PROXY === '1' && process.send) {
  process.send({ type: 'ready', port: actualPort });
}
```

Changes:
After `state.skipPermissions = skipPermissions;` at extension-handlers.ts line 516, add:
```typescript
// Notify the dev proxy so it can pass the updated value to the next worker.
if (process.env.OPENTABS_PROXY === '1' && process.send) {
  process.send({ type: 'skipPermissions', value: skipPermissions });
}
```

This must go directly in the handler (not in a callback) because the handler has direct access to the new value. The IPC message is intentionally simple — just the type and the boolean value.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify index.ts — the IPC send goes in the handler, not the startup code.
- Do NOT create a new module or abstraction for IPC messages — the pattern is simple enough to inline.
- Do NOT change the `handleConfigSetSkipPermissions` function signature or its existing behavior.
- The IPC send must happen AFTER `state.skipPermissions = skipPermissions` (line 516) but BEFORE the `sendToExtension` calls (lines 518-530) — place it immediately after line 516.

userStories.title: Dev proxy tracks skipPermissions state and passes it to new workers
userStories.description: The dev proxy should listen for { type: 'skipPermissions', value: boolean } IPC messages from the worker, track the latest value, and override the OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS env var when forking new workers.
acceptanceCriteria: The dev proxy listens for IPC messages with type 'skipPermissions' from the worker
The proxy stores the latest skipPermissions boolean value
When startWorker() forks a new worker, it passes OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS based on the tracked value ('' if false, '1' if true, omitted/passthrough if never overridden)
If skipPermissions was never changed at runtime, the env var passes through from the parent process (existing behavior preserved)

userStories.title: E2E test: skipPermissions restored state survives dev proxy hot reload
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that starts the server with OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=1, clicks 'Restore approvals' in the side panel, triggers a dev proxy hot reload, and verifies the banner does not reappear.
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts MCP server in hot mode with OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS='1'
Test opens side panel and verifies the 'Approvals skipped' banner is visible
Test clicks 'Restore approvals' and verifies the banner disappears
Test triggers a hot reload via triggerHotReload()
Test waits for 'Hot reload complete' log
Test verifies the banner is still hidden after the hot reload
Test verifies MCP tools do not have skip-permissions behavior (ask tools remain ask, not auto-promoted)
